title: Prioritize Tasks
summary: Decide what matters most when you have too much to do.
learnerPromise: Decide what matters most when you have too much to do.
whyItMatters: Not all tasks are equal. Prioritizing helps you spend energy on what actually moves you forward.
realLifeUses: Decide which homework to do first when you have multiple assignments.
Choose the most important errand when time is short.
Balance school, work, and personal commitments.
Prepare for a deadline that matters most.
youWillLearn: Tell the difference between urgent and important.
Reorder your to-do list by priority.
Focus on one high-priority task at a time.
miniChallenge: Take your to-do list and rank the top 3 by what happens if you do not do them.
steps: Look at your tasks and ask: What happens if I do not do this today?
Label each task as urgent, important, both, or neither.
Do urgent and important tasks first.
Schedule important but not urgent tasks for later.
Drop or delay tasks that are neither urgent nor important.
completionCriteria: You can explain why your top task is the most important.
You complete your highest priority task before lower ones.
commonProblems: Doing easy tasks first and ignoring hard but important ones.
Treating every task as equally urgent.
Getting distracted by new tasks that pop up.
tips: The hardest task is usually the most important. Do it first.
Ask yourself what you would do if you could only do one thing today.
It is okay to say no to tasks that do not align with your goals.
